sanskrit: तत्त्ववित्तु महाबाहो गुणकर्मविभागयोः । गुणा गुणेषु वर्तन्त इति मत्वा न सज्जते ॥
hindiTranslation: हे महाबाहो गुणविभाग और कर्मविभागको तत्त्वसे जाननेवाला महापुरुष सम्पूर्ण गुण ही गुणोंमें बरत रहे हैं  ऐसा मानकर उनमें आसक्त नहीं होता ॥
englishTranslation: The one who truly understands the division of qualities and actions does not cling, seeing that qualities act upon qualities.
essence: Seeing action as nature's movement ends attachment.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A person who understands how qualities and actions work sees that nature is doing the acting, not the separate ego. That understanding removes attachment.
simpleInsight: You can stay involved without getting entangled. Clear seeing creates freedom.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen between duty and grief. Krishna deepens the lesson: the wise person sees that the forces of nature are moving through action, not a separate doer. That vision ends clinging.
modernRelevance: You watch yourself getting pulled into a family argument, then catch the pattern before speaking. The pause changes everything: you stop defending a self-image and start seeing the forces at work.
translationLiteral: तत्त्ववित् (one who knows the truth) / तु (indeed) / महाबाहो (mighty-armed one) / गुणकर्मविभागयोः (of the division of qualities and actions) / गुणाः (the qualities) / गुणेषु (among the qualities) / वर्तन्ते (move/operate) / इति (thus) / मत्वा (having understood) / न (not) / सज्जते (becomes attached)
